title: Parse Meeting Transcripts to Markdown
tagline: Design a prompt that turns a 30-page rambling Zoom transcript into a strict Markdown table.
scenario: A product team records all their user interviews. The auto-generated transcripts are huge and full of "um", "ah", and tangents. They want an LLM to read the transcript and extract specific Feature Requests and Bug Reports into a perfectly formatted Markdown table for Notion.
brief: Write a prompt that processes a raw transcript and outputs a specific Markdown table structure, discarding all off-topic conversation.
deliverables: The strict Markdown table template (Columns: Type, Mentioned By, Quote, Summary)
Instructions to the AI on how to handle ambiguous statements (e.g., is it a bug or a feature?)
submissionGuidance: LLMs can get lazy with long texts and summarize too heavily. You must instruct it to be exhaustive in finding *every* feature/bug, but concise in how it populates the table row.

rubric.label: Markdown Strictness
rubric.description: Does the prompt clearly enforce the Markdown table syntax?

rubric.label: Signal vs Noise
rubric.description: Are the instructions clear on how to ignore tangents and focus only on bugs/features?

rubric.label: Handling Ambiguity
rubric.description: Does the prompt provide a rule for classifying vague statements?
